client_context.facility_line: Parma Pasta & Modena Olive Oil, Italian Republic
client_context.jurisdiction_label: EU & Italian Sustainability Frameworks for Food Manufacturers

executive_summary:
December 2025 brings significant compliance progress at Trivento Foods. The company's sustainability team completed its CSRD double materiality assessment in coordination with Deloitte Italy, confirming four financially material ESRS topics: E1 (climate — pasta drying kiln energy), E3 (water — Po Valley groundwater), S1 (own workforce — seasonal workforce at both facilities), and G1 (anti-corruption — olive oil supply chain). ESRS S2 (value chain workers) was assessed as not financially material at this stage, based on the Italian DOC supply chain's existing qualification frameworks.

The EU-EUDR Deforestation Regulation entered definitive applicability on 30 December 2025. While pasta durum wheat from Italian and EU sources is not a listed commodity, Trivento's Modena Olive Oil facility sources extra-virgin olive oil cuvées from Tunisian and Moroccan origins under private-label contracts. Olive oil is not currently a listed EUDR commodity; however, the Commission's Article 34 review process (due by 30 June 2028) has been signalled as likely to include olive and other Mediterranean tree crops. Trivento's supply chain team has been asked to start building origin documentation systems as a precautionary measure.

The Italian government announced in the Manovra 2026 (Law 234/2025) that the Plastic Tax implementing regulation will include a simplified reporting pathway for small-volume producers (MACSI < 5 tonnes/year). Trivento's Parma Pasta facility at 380 tonnes/year of MACSI is well above this threshold and will use the standard declaration pathway via the Agenzia delle Dogane portal.

sections.heading: Italian Plastic Tax
paragraphs: The Manovra 2026 confirmed simplified reporting pathway for MACSI producers below 5 tonnes/year. Trivento's Parma facility at 380 tonnes/year uses the standard Agenzia delle Dogane portal. Material analysis of all Parma MACSI packaging formats initiated; compostable film upgrade feasibility study commissioned from Novamont.
sections.callout: Complete Novamont compostable film upgrade feasibility assessment by 28 February 2026; register on Agenzia delle Dogane MACSI portal by 31 March 2026.

sections.heading: Corporate Sustainability Reporting Directive — DMA Completion
paragraphs: Deloitte Italy DMA completed; E1 (climate) confirmed material due to pasta drying kiln natural gas consumption (~82,000 MWh/year). Parma Pasta kiln fleet was last upgraded in 2017 and operates at 74% of current best-available-technology efficiency. ESRS E1 disclosure will require SBTi-aligned emission reduction target setting by end of FY2024 reporting cycle.
sections.callout: Engage SBTi for near-term science-based target validation in the food processing sector; submit target letter by 30 June 2026.

rows: The Manovra 2026 confirmed simplified reporting pathway for MACSI producers below 5 tonnes/year. Trivento's Parma facility at 380 tonnes/year uses the standard Agenzia delle Dogane portal. Material analysis of all Parma MACSI packaging formats initiated; compostable film upgrade feasibility study commissioned from Novamont.
Complete Novamont compostable film upgrade feasibility assessment by 28 February 2026; register on Agenzia delle Dogane MACSI portal by 31 March 2026.
